events.event_description: U.S. Legation at Honolulu established when Commissioner David Lawrence Gregg presented credentials to the Kingdom of Hawaii. Gregg was the first chief of mission to refer to the mission as a Legation.

evidence.quote: Gregg's predecessors, who served as Commissioners to the Sandwich Islands, did not refer to the Mission at Honolulu as a Legation. This term was used by Gregg, however, in his first despatch, dated December 27, 1853.

events.event_description: The U.S. Legation at Honolulu ceased to exist upon the annexation of Hawaii to the United States.

evidence.quote: Hawaii was annexed to U.S. 1898-08-12. Note: Commissioned to Hawaii. Legation Honolulu ceased to exist on August 12, 1898; Sewall thereafter used the title Special Agent.
